title: Quiz: Computational Complexity
description: Test your understanding of Big-O notation and complexity classes

questions.question: What is the time complexity of binary search?
questions.explanation: Binary search halves the search space each step, giving O(log n) complexity.

questions.question: Which complexity class contains problems that can be VERIFIED in polynomial time?
questions.explanation: NP (Nondeterministic Polynomial) contains problems where solutions can be verified in polynomial time.

questions.question: P ⊆ NP (P is a subset of NP).
questions.explanation: True! If a problem can be solved in polynomial time (P), its solution can certainly be verified in polynomial time (NP).

questions.question: What speedup does Grover's quantum algorithm provide for unstructured search?
questions.explanation: Grover's algorithm searches in O(√N) vs classical O(N) - a quadratic speedup, not exponential.

questions.question: Which problem has an EXPONENTIAL quantum speedup via Shor's algorithm?
questions.explanation: Shor's algorithm factors n-bit numbers in O(n³) quantum time, vs exponential classical time.

questions.question: Quantum computers can efficiently solve all NP-complete problems.
questions.explanation: False! There's no known quantum algorithm that solves NP-complete problems efficiently. Grover gives only quadratic speedup.

questions.question: What is BQP in quantum complexity?

options.text: Problems solvable by quantum computers in polynomial time

questions.explanation: BQP (Bounded-error Quantum Polynomial) contains problems solvable by quantum computers in polynomial time with bounded error.

questions.question: Which grows fastest as n increases?
questions.explanation: Factorial grows fastest: n! >> 2ⁿ >> n³ >> n²log n for large n. For n=20: n!=2.4×10¹⁸ vs 2ⁿ≈10⁶.
